title: kayea oblongifolia
intro: Kayea oblongifolia, also known as the Malayan kayea, is a plant species in the family Guttiferae that is native to Southeast Asia, particularly in Malaysia and Thailand. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. The health benefits of Kayea oblongifolia include its ability to aid in wound healing, reduce fever, and alleviate digestive issues. The plant is often prepared into herbal teas, infusions, and decoctions, which can be consumed to harness its medicinal properties.
